# Fixture provenance

All face-image fixtures in this directory are **public-domain** works (U.S.
federal government photographs, not subject to copyright per 17 U.S.C. 105).
They are committed so the parity tests have rights-clear inputs. Each file was
downloaded from Wikimedia Commons on **2026-06-21**, then re-encoded (EXIF
stripped, JPEG quality 90) and downscaled for size; the originals are linked
below. The golden tensor baselines (`*.gguf`) are **not** committed - they are
regenerated by `scripts/gen_baseline.py` from these images.

| Fixture | Identity | Source (Wikimedia Commons) | License |
|---------|----------|----------------------------|---------|
| `face_a.jpg` | Barack Obama (identity A) | [File:President Barack Obama.jpg](https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:President_Barack_Obama.jpg) - official 2012 presidential portrait (White House / Pete Souza) | Public domain |
| `face_b.jpg` | Barack Obama (identity A) | [File:Barack-Obama-portrait-PD.jpeg](https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Barack-Obama-portrait-PD.jpeg) - White House photo, 2009 | Public domain |
| `face_c.jpg` | Joe Biden (identity B, different) | [File:Joe Biden presidential portrait.jpg](https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Joe_Biden_presidential_portrait.jpg) - official 2021 presidential portrait (White House / Adam Schultz) | Public domain |
| `multi.jpg` | 11 faces (group) | [File:Barack Obama cabinet meeting Sept 12, 2013.jpg](https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Barack_Obama_cabinet_meeting_Sept_12,_2013.jpg) - Cabinet Room, White House (Pete Souza) | Public domain |

## Direct download URLs (originals, before resize)

- `face_a.jpg`: https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/8/8d/President_Barack_Obama.jpg
- `face_b.jpg`: https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/b/b2/Barack-Obama-portrait-PD.jpeg
- `face_c.jpg`: https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/6/68/Joe_Biden_presidential_portrait.jpg
- `multi.jpg`: https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/d/d2/Barack_Obama_cabinet_meeting_Sept_12%2C_2013.jpg

## Why these images

- `face_a` and `face_b` are the **same identity** (Barack Obama) from two
  different photo sessions, so the verify gate exercises a non-trivial positive
  match (reference insightface cosine ~0.69).
- `face_c` is a **different identity** (Joe Biden); against `face_a`/`face_b` the
  reference cosine is ~ -0.05 (clear negative), giving a wide same/different
  margin for the verify gate.
- `multi.jpg` contains **11 detectable faces**, used as the detection golden set.

## Regenerating the baselines

```bash
.venv/bin/python scripts/gen_baseline.py --model buffalo_l \
  --image tests/fixtures/face_a.jpg --output /tmp/fd_baseline.gguf
```

The dumped GGUF holds `det_boxes`, `det_scores`, `det_landmarks`,
`aligned_crop` (112x112x3 RGB), `embedding` (512-d L2-normalized), and
`age`/`gender` scalars, read by the C++ parity tests via `fdtest::load_baseline`
(env `FACEDETECT_TEST_BASELINE`). If the insightface deps or the buffalo_l pack
are absent, the script exits 2 with `FACEDETECT_BASELINE_DEPS_MISSING` and the
parity tests skip (ctest return 77).
